properties.NAME: Fort Canning Lighthouse
properties.DESCRIPTION: Originally built in 1855, the lighthouse guided ships into the Singapore harbour.

properties.NAME: Tomb of Iskandar Shah
properties.DESCRIPTION: Believed to be the burial site of the last ruler of the ancient Kingdom of Singapura.

properties.NAME: Former Ford Factory
properties.DESCRIPTION: Site of the British surrender to the Japanese on 15 February 1942.

properties.NAME: Istana Kampong Glam
properties.DESCRIPTION: Former royal residence of the Malay Sultan, built in 1843.

properties.NAME: Goodwood Park Hotel
properties.DESCRIPTION: Built in 1900, originally the Teutonia Club for the German community. National Monument.

properties.NAME: Emerald Hill Shophouses
properties.DESCRIPTION: Well-preserved Peranakan terrace houses dating from the 1900s-1920s along Emerald Hill Road.

properties.NAME: Sri Thendayuthapani Temple
properties.DESCRIPTION: Hindu temple built in 1859, gazetted as a National Monument in 2014.

properties.NAME: Tan Yeok Nee House
properties.DESCRIPTION: Built in 1885, one of the last surviving Chinese mansions in traditional southern Chinese architectural style.

properties.NAME: House of Tan Teng Niah
properties.DESCRIPTION: Last surviving Chinese villa in Little India, built in 1900.

properties.NAME: National Museum of Singapore
properties.DESCRIPTION: Oldest museum in Singapore, established in 1887. National Monument.

properties.NAME: Armenian Church
properties.DESCRIPTION: Oldest surviving church in Singapore, designed by George Drumgoole Coleman in 1835.

properties.NAME: Raffles Hotel
properties.DESCRIPTION: Established in 1887 by the Sarkies Brothers. National Monument since 1987.
